mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with trade-offs including the need for specific hardware optimizations and configurations.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM easily.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.19.1

```

> This command adds the vLLM Helm repository.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function correctly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.19.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace using Helm, ensuring that the correct version is deployed.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.19.1

```

> This command installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for vLLM, which is necessary to define the model and its configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This command creates an example vLLM resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM deployment to ensure that it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the vLLM instance and any data associated with it.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step will upgrade the vLLM installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vllms -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.19.1

kubectl get vllms -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resource is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllms -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM resource may not have been created or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the namespace and ensure the vLLM resource is created correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD installation may have failed due to network issues or incorrect URLs.

**Fix:**
Re-run the CRD installation command and check for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to insufficient permissions or incorrect Helm chart values.

**Fix:**
Check your Helm permissions and review the values used during installation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm pods are in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the vLLM pods are failing to start, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old model configuration format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your model configurations to the new format as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resource listed with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get vllms -n vllm'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
